creator: bscott@bunyatech.com.au
text:
Possibly more informative backtrace from newer kgdb:

(kgdb) bt
#0  get_curthread () at ./machine/pcpu.h:66
#1  doadump (textdump=0) at /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_shutdown.c:366
#2  0xffff0000000d72a0 in db_dump (dummy=<optimized out>, dummy2=false, dummy3=0, dummy4=0x0) at /usr/src/sys/ddb/db_command.c:574
#3  0xffff0000000d7078 in db_command (last_cmdp=<optimized out>, cmd_table=<optimized out>, dopager=<optimized out>) at /usr/src/sys/ddb/db_command.c:481
#4  0xffff0000000d6e3c in db_command_loop () at /usr/src/sys/ddb/db_command.c:534
#5  0xffff0000000d9f74 in db_trap (type=<optimized out>, code=<optimized out>) at /usr/src/sys/ddb/db_main.c:252
#6  0xffff0000003cabb4 in kdb_trap (type=37, code=0, tf=0xffff000051744530) at /usr/src/sys/kern/subr_kdb.c:697
#7  0xffff000000692bc0 in data_abort (td=0xfffffd00011b2000, frame=0xffff000051744530, esr=2516582406, far=<optimized out>, lower=0) at /usr/src/sys/arm64/arm64/trap.c:261
#8  0xffff0000006928dc in do_el1h_sync (td=0xfffffd00011b2000, frame=0xffff000051744530) at /usr/src/sys/arm64/arm64/trap.c:341
#9  <signal handler called>
#10 ck_pr_md_load_ptr (target=0xffff000054dc25e8) at /usr/src/sys/contrib/ck/include/gcc/aarch64/ck_pr.h:114
#11 dyn_free_states (chain=<optimized out>) at /usr/src/sys/netpfil/ipfw/ip_fw_dynamic.c:2037
#12 dyn_tick (vnetx=0x0) at /usr/src/sys/netpfil/ipfw/ip_fw_dynamic.c:2631
#13 0xffff00000039e0d0 in softclock_call_cc (c=0xffff000054112250 <dyn_timeout>, cc=<optimized out>, direct=<optimized out>) at /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_timeout.c:731
#14 0xffff00000039e430 in softclock (arg=0xffff000000ce9280 <cc_cpu>) at /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_timeout.c:869
#15 0xffff00000034a17c in intr_event_execute_handlers (p=<optimized out>, ie=0xfffffd0001165c00) at /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_intr.c:1013
#16 0xffff00000034a824 in ithread_execute_handlers (ie=<optimized out>, p=<optimized out>) at /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_intr.c:1026
#17 ithread_loop (arg=0xfffffd00011a9020) at /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_intr.c:1106
#18 0xffff0000003473e8 in fork_exit (callout=0xffff00000034a770 <ithread_loop>, arg=0xfffffd00011a9020, frame=0xffff000051744960) at /usr/src/sys/kern/kern_fork.c:1039
#19 <signal handler called>
(kgdb) x 0xffff000054dc25e8
0xffff000054dc25e8:	Cannot access memory at address 0xffff000054dc25e8
(kgdb)

Without knowing any aarch64 assembler, it looks like
#10 ck_pr_md_load_ptr (target=0xffff000054dc25e8)
is called with an invalid pointer and thats where things blow up. The address doesn't look unreasonable in structure so could just be an out of bounds thing. This is loading up the per CPU state in dyn_free_states in the DYNSTATE_GET macro:

CPU_FOREACH(i) {
       dyn_hp_cache[cached_count] = DYNSTATE_GET(i);
       if (dyn_hp_cache[cached_count] != NULL)
           cached_count++;
}

My debugging skills are stopped by the optimised out values all over the place, so any guidance would be appreciated.
